summary: E17: .github/workflows/deploy.yml ‚Äî lint‚Üítype-check‚Üítest‚Üíbuild‚Üíappleboy/ssh-action runs deploy.sh <sha>; secrets VPS_HOST + DEPLOY_SSH_KEY
description:
GitHub Actions workflow at .github/workflows/deploy.yml. Triggers on push to main. Steps: lint ‚Üí type-check ‚Üí test ‚Üí build (CI build is a gate, not the artifact ‚Äî VPS rebuilds from SHA via deploy.sh) ‚Üí SSH to VPS ‚Üí run /srv/guru-web/deploy.sh <sha>.

ACCESS PATTERN (decided 2026-04-22): CI reaches VPS via TAILSCALE, not public SSH. UFW on the VPS allows port 22 only on tailscale0, by design.

Implementation:
- Use tailscale/github-action@v3 early in the job to join the runner to the tailnet as an ephemeral node.
- Auth via TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY GitHub secret. Key is generated in Tailscale admin ‚Üí Settings ‚Üí Keys as:
    * reusable: true (CI runs many times)
    * ephemeral: true (node auto-cleans up after run)
    * pre-approved: true (no manual admit per run)
    * tags: tag:ci (required for reusable+ephemeral; also lets ACLs restrict CI‚Üíserver access)
    * expiry: 90 days (rotate before; calendar reminder)
- ACL in Tailscale admin should allow tag:ci ‚Üí tag:guru-web-prod (or similar) on port 22 only. VPS gets tag:guru-web-prod assigned when it joins.
- Then standard appleboy/ssh-action@v1 or plain ssh command to connect to guru-web-prod (tailnet hostname resolves via MagicDNS).

Secrets required in GitHub repo settings:
- TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY ‚Äî the ephemeral+reusable key above
- DEPLOY_SSH_KEY ‚Äî private key for deploy@guru-web-prod (pubkey lives on VPS at /home/deploy/.ssh/authorized_keys, populated by bootstrap from DEPLOY_PUBKEY in /etc/guru-bootstrap.env)

Depends on D13 (bbe07228) being complete ‚Äî the workflow just invokes deploy.sh.
